mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns are positive in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE growth exceeds 0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with decelerating inflation indicates robust consumer demand without monetary tightening pressure, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production reflects immediate supply-side contraction and reduced corporate output, leading to negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of declining INDPRO months, or the signal fails to predict returns during stable supply regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, borrowing costs for corporations and households become restrictive enough to suppress earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in >60% of periods above the threshold, or the predictive IC drops below zero when rates stabilize above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >65% of months with positive payroll growth, or the signal shows no predictive power in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: High housing starts combined with expanding real GDP signal strong domestic demand and economic momentum, triggering positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in >60% of triggered cases, or housing starts lose predictive relevance when GDP growth falls below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in industrial production indicates immediate supply chain disruptions and factory slowdowns, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in >65% of triggered months, or the signal fails to predict returns when declines are driven by seasonal adjustments.
